description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 strategy/plan/coverage cluster: product-level strategy vs per-change test plan vs coverage audit vs automation architecture, plus discrimination against the shipped full-codebase-auditor.

cases.prompt: How should we test this product overall — what layers do we need, what must pass before a release, and who owns what?
cases.reason: Product-wide layer decisions, release gates, and ownership are strategy; no single feature, no existing-coverage audit, no tooling design was asked for.

cases.prompt: Design the tests for the new bulk-invite feature: what we'll test, with what data, and when we're done.
cases.reason: One feature's scope, data, and exit criteria is a test plan instantiating the strategy, not the strategy itself.

cases.prompt: Which of our critical paths have no tests today? Map what's covered and what isn't.
cases.reason: Auditing what existing tests cover is the mapper; strategy defines what SHOULD exist.

cases.prompt: Give me an overall health audit of this repository — dead code, duplication, dependency hygiene, debt map.
cases.reason: Whole-repo health is the shipped full-codebase-auditor; this cluster governs testing only.
